subject: Physics
q: A solid disc of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> and mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> is rotating with an angular velocity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>600</mn><mo> </mo><mi>rpm</mi></math>, about an axis normal to its circular plane and passing through its centre of mass. The retarding torque required to bring the disc at rest in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> is _________<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>π</mi><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>
question: A solid disc of radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi></math> and mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> is rotating with an angular velocity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>600</mn><mo> </mo><mi>rpm</mi></math>, about an axis normal to its circular plane and passing through its centre of mass. The retarding torque required to bring the disc at rest in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> is _________<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>π</mi><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>
solution:
τ=ΔLΔt=Iωf-ωiΔt

τ=mR22×[0-ω]Δt

=10×20×10-222×600×π30×10

=0.4π=4π×10-1

Moment of inertia M.I. of four bodies, having same mass and radius, are reported as;

I1=M.I. of thin circular ring about its diameter,

I2=M.I. of circular disc about an axis perpendicular to disc and going through the centre,

I3=M.I. of solid cylinder about its axis and

I4=M.I. of solid sphere about its diameter.

Then:
question:
Moment of inertia M.I. of four bodies, having same mass and radius, are reported as;

I1=M.I. of thin circular ring about its diameter,

I2=M.I. of circular disc about an axis perpendicular to disc and going through the centre,

I3=M.I. of solid cylinder about its axis and

I4=M.I. of solid sphere about its diameter.

Then:
solution:
Ring I1=MR22 about diameter

Disc I2=MR22

Solid cylinder I3=MR22

Solid sphere I4=25MR2

I1=I2=I3>I4

subject: Physics
q: A solid sphere ($A$) of mass $5m$ and a spherical shell ($B$) of mass $m$, both having same radius, are placed on a rough surface. When a force of same magnitude is applied tangentially at the highest points of $A$ and $B$, they start rolling without slipping with an acceleration of $a_A$ and $a_B$, respectively. The ratio of $a_A$ and $a_B$ is __________.
question: A solid sphere ($A$) of mass $5m$ and a spherical shell ($B$) of mass $m$, both having same radius, are placed on a rough surface. When a force of same magnitude is applied tangentially at the highest points of $A$ and $B$, they start rolling without slipping with an acceleration of $a_A$ and $a_B$, respectively. The ratio of $a_A$ and $a_B$ is __________.
solution:
For a body of mass $M$ and radius $R$ rolling without slipping under a force $F$ applied tangentially at the highest point, the torque about the point of contact with the ground is $\tau = F(2R)$.

The moment of inertia about the point of contact is $I = I_{cm} + MR^2$.

Using $\tau = I\alpha$ and the rolling without slipping condition $a = \alpha R$, we get:

$F(2R) = (I_{cm} + MR^2) \dfrac{a}{R} \Rightarrow a = \dfrac{2FR^2}{I_{cm} + MR^2}$

For the solid sphere $A$ of mass $5m$:

$I_{cm,A} = \dfrac{2}{5}(5m)R^2 = 2mR^2$

$a_A = \dfrac{2FR^2}{2mR^2 + 5mR^2} = \dfrac{2F}{7m}$

For the spherical shell $B$ of mass $m$:

$I_{cm,B} = \dfrac{2}{3}mR^2$

$a_B = \dfrac{2FR^2}{\dfrac{2}{3}mR^2 + mR^2} = \dfrac{2F}{\dfrac{5}{3}m} = \dfrac{6F}{5m}$

The ratio of their accelerations is:

$\dfrac{a_A}{a_B} = \dfrac{\dfrac{2F}{7m}}{\dfrac{6F}{5m}} = \dfrac{2}{7} \times \dfrac{5}{6} = \dfrac{5}{21}$

Answer: $5 : 21$

subject: Physics
q: Two coaxial discs, having moments of inertia <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>I</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msub><mi>I</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> , are rotating with respective angular velocities <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">ω</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><mi> </mi><mfrac><mrow><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">ω</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac></math> , about their common axis. They are brought in contact with each other and thereafter they rotate with a common angular velocity. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>E</mi><mi>f</mi></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>E</mi><mi>i</mi></msub></math> are the final and initial total energies, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>E</mi><mi>f</mi></msub><mo>-</mo><msub><mi>E</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">i</mi></msub></mrow></mfenced></math> is:
question: Two coaxial discs, having moments of inertia <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>I</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><msub><mi>I</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math> , are rotating with respective angular velocities <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">ω</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><mi> </mi><mfrac><mrow><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">ω</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></mfrac></math> , about their common axis. They are brought in contact with each other and thereafter they rotate with a common angular velocity. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>E</mi><mi>f</mi></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>E</mi><mi>i</mi></msub></math> are the final and initial total energies, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><msub><mi>E</mi><mi>f</mi></msub><mo>-</mo><msub><mi>E</mi><mi mathvariant="italic">i</mi></msub></mrow></mfenced></math> is:
solution:
As external torque is zero, angular momentum remains conserved.
I1ω1+I12ω12=I1+I12ω
∴ Common angular velocity ω=5ω16
∴ Ef-Ei=12×I1+I12ω2-12×I1ω1 2-12×Il2ω122
=12×3I1256ω12-916I1ω12
=75 I1ω12-81 I1ω12144
=-6I1ω12144
=-I1ω1224

subject: Physics
q: A ring and a solid sphere rotating about an axis passing through their centres have same radii of gyration. The axis of rotation is perpendicular to plane of ring. The ratio of radius of ring to that of sphere is 2x. The value of x is _____.
question: A ring and a solid sphere rotating about an axis passing through their centres have same radii of gyration. The axis of rotation is perpendicular to plane of ring. The ratio of radius of ring to that of sphere is 2x. The value of x is _____.
solution:
The moment of inertia of the ring about an axis perpendicular to the plane of ring is Ir=mk2.

The moment of inertia of the sphere is 

Is=25mR2s

It is given that 

mk2=mR2r=25mRs2   ...(i)

From equation (i)

Rr=25Rs

From the question, it can be written that

RrRs=2x

⇒RrRs=25⇒x=5

subject: Physics
q: Moment of inertia of a rod of mass ' M ' and length 'L' about an axis passing through its center and normal to its length is ' $\alpha$ '. Now the rod is cut into two equal parts and these parts are joined symmetrically to form a cross shape. Moment of inertia of cross about an axis passing through its center and normal to plane containing cross is :
question: Moment of inertia of a rod of mass ' M ' and length 'L' about an axis passing through its center and normal to its length is ' $\alpha$ '. Now the rod is cut into two equal parts and these parts are joined symmetrically to form a cross shape. Moment of inertia of cross about an axis passing through its center and normal to plane containing cross is :
solution:
$\alpha=\frac{\mathrm{M} \ell^2}{12}$
$\begin{aligned} & \alpha^{\prime}=2\left[\frac{\frac{\mathrm{M}}{2}\left(\frac{\ell}{2}\right)^2}{12}\right] \\ & \alpha^{\prime}=\frac{\mathrm{M} \ell^2}{48}=\frac{\alpha}{4}\end{aligned}$
Correct option is (2)

subject: Physics
q: A disc of radius R and mass M is rolling horizontally without slipping with speed v. It then moves up an inclined smooth surface as shown in figure. The maximum height that the disc can go up the incline is:
question: A disc of radius R and mass M is rolling horizontally without slipping with speed v. It then moves up an inclined smooth surface as shown in figure. The maximum height that the disc can go up the incline is:
solution:
Only the translational kinetic energy of disc changes into gravitational potential energy and rotational KE remains unchanged as there is no friction.

12mv2=mgh

⇒h=v22g

solution:
Ml212+Ml42=MK2
l212+l216=K2
K=748l

subject: Physics
q: A thin circular disc of mass $M$ and radius $R$ is rotating in a horizontal plane about an axis passing through its centre and perpendicular to its plane with angular velocity $\omega$. If another disc of same dimensions but of mass $\mathrm{M} / 2$ is placed gently on the first disc co-axially, then the new angular velocity of the system is :
question: A thin circular disc of mass $M$ and radius $R$ is rotating in a horizontal plane about an axis passing through its centre and perpendicular to its plane with angular velocity $\omega$. If another disc of same dimensions but of mass $\mathrm{M} / 2$ is placed gently on the first disc co-axially, then the new angular velocity of the system is :
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{I}_1 \omega=\mathrm{I}_2 \omega_2 \\ & \frac{\mathrm{MR}^2}{2} \omega=\frac{3}{2}\left(\frac{\mathrm{MR}^2}{2}\right) \omega_2 \\ & \omega_2=\frac{2}{3} \omega\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
q: Two identical solid spheres each of mass 2 kg and radii 10 cm are fixed at the ends of a light rod. The separation between the centres of the spheres is 40 cm. The moment of inertia of the system about an axis perpendicular to the rod passing through its middle point is______×10–3 kg m2.
question: Two identical solid spheres each of mass 2 kg and radii 10 cm are fixed at the ends of a light rod. The separation between the centres of the spheres is 40 cm. The moment of inertia of the system about an axis perpendicular to the rod passing through its middle point is______×10–3 kg m2.
solution:
The moment of inertia of a sphere is given by the formula I=25MR2.

Since there are two masses, the total moment of inertia about the axis perpendicular to the rod passing through the centre is 

I=2×25MR2+Ml2+r2   ...(i)

The given data is 

M=2 kgl=0.4-0.2 m=0.2 mR=0.1 m

Substituting the values in equation (i)

I=45(2×0.12)+2×2(0.22+0.1)2  kg m2⇒I=45×0.02+4×0.04  kg m2⇒I=0.016+0.16  kg m2=0.176 kg m2

subject: Physics
q: A force of $40 \mathrm{~N}$ acts on a point $\mathrm{B}$ at the end of an L-shaped object, as shown in the figure. The angle $\theta$ that will produce maximum moment of the force about point $\mathrm{A}$ is given by:
question: A force of $40 \mathrm{~N}$ acts on a point $\mathrm{B}$ at the end of an L-shaped object, as shown in the figure. The angle $\theta$ that will produce maximum moment of the force about point $\mathrm{A}$ is given by:
solution:
To produce maximum moment of force line of action of force must be perpendicular to line $\mathrm{AB}$.
$\therefore \tan \theta=\frac{2}{4}=\frac{1}{2}$

q:
Electric field of a plane electromagnetic wave propagating through a non-magnetic medium is given by E=20cos2×1010t-200x V m-1. The dielectric constant of the medium is equal to:
(Take μr=1 )
question:
Electric field of a plane electromagnetic wave propagating through a non-magnetic medium is given by E=20cos2×1010t-200x V m-1. The dielectric constant of the medium is equal to:
(Take μr=1 )
solution:
Equate the wave equation with the equation of EM wave, E=E0cosωt-kx V m-1 here the given equation of EM wave,  E=20cos2×1010t-200x V m-1⇒v=ωk=2×1010200=108 m s-1
The refractive index,⇒n=cv=3×108108⇒3=μrεr  μr=1given)
εr=9

subject: Physics
q: Moment of inertia of a square plate of side l about the axis passing through one of the corner and perpendicular to the plane of square plate is given by:
question: Moment of inertia of a square plate of side l about the axis passing through one of the corner and perpendicular to the plane of square plate is given by:
solution:
Use parallel axis theorem to get the magnitude of the moment of inertia of a square plate of side l about the axis passing through one of the corners and perpendicular to the plane of a square plate, 

Iz=Icm+Ml22

⇒Iz=Ml26+Ml22=4Ml26=2Ml23.

subject: Physics
q: For rolling spherical shell, the ratio of rotational kinetic energy and total kinetic energy is x5. The value of x is _____.
question: For rolling spherical shell, the ratio of rotational kinetic energy and total kinetic energy is x5. The value of x is _____.
solution:
The rotational kinetic energy is given by 

Krot=Iω22=13MR2ω2.

The total kinetic energy is given by 

Ktot=12mv2+13MR2ω2=mR2ω22+13MR2ω2=56MR2ω2

Thus, the ratio is 

KrotKtot=13MR2ω256MR2ω2=25

Comparing with the given value,

x5=25⇒x=2

subject: Physics
q: A uniform circular disc of radius ' R ' and mass ' M ' is rotating about an axis perpendicular to its plane and passing through its centre. A small circular part of radius $\mathrm{R} / 2$ is removed from the original disc as shown in the figure. Find the moment of inertia of the remaining part of the original disc about the axis as given above.
question: A uniform circular disc of radius ' R ' and mass ' M ' is rotating about an axis perpendicular to its plane and passing through its centre. A small circular part of radius $\mathrm{R} / 2$ is removed from the original disc as shown in the figure. Find the moment of inertia of the remaining part of the original disc about the axis as given above.
solution: $\begin{aligned} & I_{\text {complete }}=\frac{M R^2}{2} \\ & \begin{aligned} I_{\text {removed }} & =\left(\frac{M}{4}\right)\left(\frac{R}{2}\right)^2 \frac{1}{2}+\left(\frac{M}{4}\right)\left(\frac{R}{2}\right)^2 \\ & =\frac{3 M R^2}{32} \\ I=\frac{M R^2}{2} & -\frac{3 M R^2}{32}=\frac{13}{32} M R^2\end{aligned}\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
q:
A force F→=4i^+3j^+4k^ is applied on an intersection point of x=2 plane and x-axis. The magnitude of torque of this force about a point 2,3,4 is ________.

(Round off to the Nearest Integer)
question:
A force F→=4i^+3j^+4k^ is applied on an intersection point of x=2 plane and x-axis. The magnitude of torque of this force about a point 2,3,4 is ________.

(Round off to the Nearest Integer)
solution:
τ→=r→×F→

r→=2i^-2i^+3j^+4k^=-3j^-4k^

and F→=4i^+3j^+4k^

τ→=r→×F→=i^j^k^0-3-4434

=i^-12+12-j^0+16+k^0+12

=-16i^+12k^

∴ |τ→|=162+122=20

q: A mass m is supported by a massless string wound around a uniform hollow cylinder of mass m and radius R. If the string does not slip on the cylinder, with what acceleration will the mass fall on release?
question: A mass m is supported by a massless string wound around a uniform hollow cylinder of mass m and radius R. If the string does not slip on the cylinder, with what acceleration will the mass fall on release?
solution:
mg - T = ma      ----- 1

TR = Iα

TR =mR 2 α

​∴T=mRα   ----2

For no slippping

a = Rα  ---3

putting (3) in (2) 

T = ma ---4

Adding 1 and 4

mg = 2ma

∴   a = g 2

q: A triangular plate is shown. A force F→=4i^-3j^ is applied at point P. The torque at point P with respect to point O and Q are:
question: A triangular plate is shown. A force F→=4i^-3j^ is applied at point P. The torque at point P with respect to point O and Q are:
solution:
F→=4i^-3j^

r→1=5i^+53j^ and r→2=-5i^+53j^

Torque about 'O'

τ→O=r→1×F→=-15-203k^

Torque about 'Q'

τ→Q=r→2×F→=15-203k^

subject: Physics
q: A uniform sphere of mass <math><mn>500 </mn><mi></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> rolls without slipping on a plane horizontal surface with its centre moving at a speed of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5.00</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. Its kinetic energy is:
question: A uniform sphere of mass <math><mn>500 </mn><mi></mi><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi></math> rolls without slipping on a plane horizontal surface with its centre moving at a speed of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5.00</mn><mo> </mo><mi>cm</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. Its kinetic energy is:
solution:
K.E of the sphere = Translational K.E+ Rotational K.E
=12mv21+K2R2 ,K= Radius of gyration
= 12×12×210021+25
= 354×10-4 J
= 8.75×10-4 J

subject: Physics
q: As shown in the figure, a bob of mass m is tied to a massless string whose other end portion is wound on a fly wheel (disc) of radius r and mass m. When released from rest the bob starts falling vertically. When it has covered a distance of h, the angular speed of the wheel will be:
question: As shown in the figure, a bob of mass m is tied to a massless string whose other end portion is wound on a fly wheel (disc) of radius r and mass m. When released from rest the bob starts falling vertically. When it has covered a distance of h, the angular speed of the wheel will be:
solution:
Using work-energy theorem 

mgh=12mv2+12Iω2

mgh=12mω2r2+12mr22ω2    ( since I=mr22 and v=rω )

mgh=34mω2r2

Hence, ω=4gh3r2=1r4gh3

subject: Physics
q: A heavy iron bar, of weight $W$ is having its one end on the ground and the other on the shoulder of a person. The bar makes an angle $\theta$ with the horizontal. The weight experienced by the person is :
question: A heavy iron bar, of weight $W$ is having its one end on the ground and the other on the shoulder of a person. The bar makes an angle $\theta$ with the horizontal. The weight experienced by the person is :
solution: $R=$ net reaction force by shoulder Balancing torque about pt of contact on ground: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{W}\left(\frac{\mathrm{L}}{2} \cos \theta\right)=\mathrm{R}(\mathrm{L} \cos \theta) \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{R}=\frac{\mathrm{W}}{2}\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
q: A circular plate is rotating in horizontal plane, about an axis passing through its centre and perpendicular to the plate, with an angular velocity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>ω</mi></math>. A person sits at the centre having two dumbbells in his hands. When he stretched out his hands, the moment of inertia of the system becomes triple. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi></math> be the initial Kinetic energy of the system, then final Kinetic energy will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>E</mi><mi>x</mi></mfrac></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is
question: A circular plate is rotating in horizontal plane, about an axis passing through its centre and perpendicular to the plate, with an angular velocity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>ω</mi></math>. A person sits at the centre having two dumbbells in his hands. When he stretched out his hands, the moment of inertia of the system becomes triple. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi></math> be the initial Kinetic energy of the system, then final Kinetic energy will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>E</mi><mi>x</mi></mfrac></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is
solution:
The kinetic energy is given by 

E=12Iω2=L22I

It is given that the new moment of inertia

 I'=3I 

Clearly, the final kinetic energy is

Ef=L22I'=L26I⇒Ef=E3

subject: Physics
q:
Match List-I with List-II

  List-I   List-II
(A) Moment of inertia of solid sphere
of radius R about any tangent.		 (I) 53MR2
(B) Moment of inertia of hollow sphere of radius
R about any tangent.		 (II) 75MR2
(C) Moment of inertia of circular ring of radius
R about its diameter.		 (III) 14MR2
(D) Moment of inertia of circular disc of radius
R about any diameter.		 (IV) 12MR2
question:
Match List-I with List-II

  List-I   List-II
(A) Moment of inertia of solid sphere
of radius R about any tangent.		 (I) 53MR2
(B) Moment of inertia of hollow sphere of radius
R about any tangent.		 (II) 75MR2
(C) Moment of inertia of circular ring of radius
R about its diameter.		 (III) 14MR2
(D) Moment of inertia of circular disc of radius
R about any diameter.		 (IV) 12MR2
solution:
For solid sphere

I0=Icom +MR2  (Using parallel axis theorem)

I0=25MR2+MR2

I0=75MR2⇒A→II

For hollow sphere

I=Icom+MR2⇒I=53MR2⇒B→I

For Circular ring

I1+I2=I3 ( Using perpendicular axis theorem)

By symmetry MOI

About 1 and 2 Axis are same i.e.

I1=I2

∴  2I1=I3=MR2   ∵Icom=MR2

I1=MR22⇒ C→IV

Similarly in disc

2I1=MR22∵Icom=MR22

I1=MR24⇒D→III

subject: Physics
q: Three equal masses $m$ are kept at vertices (A, B, C) of an equilateral triangle of side a in free space. At $t=0$, they are given an initial velocity $\vec{V}_A=V_0 \overrightarrow{A C}, \vec{V}_B=V_0 \overrightarrow{B A}$ and $\vec{V}_C=V_0 \overrightarrow{C B}$. Here, $\overrightarrow{A C}, \overrightarrow{C B}$ and $\overrightarrow{B A}$ are unit vectors along the edges of the triangle. If the three masses interact gravitationally, then the magnitude of the net angular momentum of the system at the point of collision is :
question: Three equal masses $m$ are kept at vertices (A, B, C) of an equilateral triangle of side a in free space. At $t=0$, they are given an initial velocity $\vec{V}_A=V_0 \overrightarrow{A C}, \vec{V}_B=V_0 \overrightarrow{B A}$ and $\vec{V}_C=V_0 \overrightarrow{C B}$. Here, $\overrightarrow{A C}, \overrightarrow{C B}$ and $\overrightarrow{B A}$ are unit vectors along the edges of the triangle. If the three masses interact gravitationally, then the magnitude of the net angular momentum of the system at the point of collision is :
solution: $d=\frac{a}{2 \sqrt{3}}$ Angular momentum of one mass about point $O$ $\begin{aligned} L & =m v d \\ & =m v_0 \cdot \frac{a}{2 \sqrt{3}} \end{aligned}$ Net angular momentum about point $O$ $\begin{aligned} L_{\text {net }} & =32 \\ & =\frac{\sqrt{3} m v_0 a}{2} \end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
q: A solid sphere of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> rolls without slipping on a plane surface. Its kinetic energy is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi></math>. The speed of the centre of mass of the sphere is ______ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cm</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>.
question: A solid sphere of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> rolls without slipping on a plane surface. Its kinetic energy is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi></math>. The speed of the centre of mass of the sphere is ______ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cm</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>.
solution:
Let velocity of centre of mass of sphere be v.

Total kinetic energy of solid sphere is KE=12mv2+12Iω2, where, I is moment of inertia of the solid sphere about the axis passing through it's centre of mass and ω=vR pure rolling condition.

Therefore,

 7×10-3=12×1×(v)2+1225(1)R2vR2

⇒12v275=7×10-3

⇒v2=10-2

⇒v=10-1=0.1 m s-1=10 cm s-1

q: A ball of mass 10 kg moving with a velocity 103 m s-1 along X-axis, hits another ball of mass 20 kg which is at rest. After the collision, the first ball comes to rest and the second one disintegrates into two equal pieces. One of the pieces starts moving along Y-axis at a speed of 10 m s-1. The second piece starts moving at a speed of 20 m s-1 at an angle θ (degree) with respect to the X-axis. The configuration of pieces after the collision is shown in the figure. The value of $\theta$ to the nearest integer is _________.
question: A ball of mass 10 kg moving with a velocity 103 m s-1 along X-axis, hits another ball of mass 20 kg which is at rest. After the collision, the first ball comes to rest and the second one disintegrates into two equal pieces. One of the pieces starts moving along Y-axis at a speed of 10 m s-1. The second piece starts moving at a speed of 20 m s-1 at an angle θ (degree) with respect to the X-axis. The configuration of pieces after the collision is shown in the figure. The value of $\theta$ to the nearest integer is _________.
solution:
Before Collision 

After Collision 

From conservation of momentum along x axis;

P→i=P→f

10×103=200cosθ

cosθ=32

θ=30°

subject: Physics
q: A solid cylinder having radius $R$ and length $L$ is slipping on a rough horizontal plane. At time $t=0$ the cylinder has a translational velocity $v_0=49$ m/s, perpendicular to its axis and a rotational velocity $v_0/4R$ about the centre. The time taken by the cylinder to start rolling is ________ seconds. (coefficient of kinetic friction $\mu_K=0.25$ and $g=9.8$ m/s$^2$)
question: A solid cylinder having radius $R$ and length $L$ is slipping on a rough horizontal plane. At time $t=0$ the cylinder has a translational velocity $v_0=49$ m/s, perpendicular to its axis and a rotational velocity $v_0/4R$ about the centre. The time taken by the cylinder to start rolling is ________ seconds. (coefficient of kinetic friction $\mu_K=0.25$ and $g=9.8$ m/s$^2$)
solution:
Initial translational velocity, $v_0 = 49$ m/s.

Initial angular velocity, $\omega_0 = \dfrac{v_0}{4R}$.

Velocity of the lowest point of the cylinder is $v_{bottom} = v_0 - \omega_0 R = v_0 - \dfrac{v_0}{4} = \dfrac{3v_0}{4} > 0$.

Since the lowest point is slipping forward, kinetic friction $f_k = \mu_k m g$ acts in the backward direction.

Translational acceleration, $a = -\dfrac{f_k}{m} = -\mu_k g$.

Angular acceleration, $\alpha = \dfrac{f_k R}{I} = \dfrac{\mu_k m g R}{\dfrac{1}{2} m R^2} = \dfrac{2\mu_k g}{R}$.

At time $t$, the translational velocity is $v = v_0 - \mu_k g t$.

The angular velocity is $\omega = \omega_0 + \alpha t = \dfrac{v_0}{4R} + \dfrac{2\mu_k g}{R} t$.

For pure rolling to start, $v = \omega R$.

$v_0 - \mu_k g t = \left( \dfrac{v_0}{4R} + \dfrac{2\mu_k g}{R} t \right) R$

$v_0 - \mu_k g t = \dfrac{v_0}{4} + 2\mu_k g t$

$3\mu_k g t = \dfrac{3v_0}{4}$

$t = \dfrac{v_0}{4\mu_k g}$

Substituting the given values $v_0 = 49$ m/s, $\mu_k = 0.25$, and $g = 9.8$ m/s$^2$:

$t = \dfrac{49}{4 \times 0.25 \times 9.8} = \dfrac{49}{9.8} = 5$ s.

Answer: $5$
